mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand strength, supporting higher corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction term loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates improving supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant during expansion phases, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 310.0 reflect entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 310.0, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and unemployment rate declines confirm robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is non-significant (p > 0.05) in predictive models.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts reflect strong residential investment and consumer confidence, which translates into higher durable goods demand and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of observations when HOUST > 1300.

mechanism_narrative: High industrial production coinciding with elevated consumer prices signals supply-constrained inflation, which raises recession risks and forces aggressive monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of observations when both conditions are met, or the interaction term fails to predict returns in walk-forward validation.
